phases.name: Phase 1 — ms_fba_util per-model tests + Biolog stash

parallel_group.prompt:
Implement Phase 1 of the Template Evaluation Suite PRD. READ the PRD first: agent-io/prds/template-evaluation-suite/fullprompt.md (Implementation Decisions, the 'Confront-resolved specifications (round 1)' subsection, and the numbered Acceptance Criteria are binding). All work in this phase is in src/kbutillib/ms_fba_utils.py plus a committed data file and tests.

Add these seven per-model functions to MSFBAUtils (each accepts a cobra Model or MSModelUtil via the existing _check_and_convert_model convention):
1. classify_reactions_by_fva(model, media=None, essential_fraction=0.2) — two passes via the EXISTING run_fva (never cobra.flux_variability_analysis, which is broken): unconstrained pass (fraction_of_optimum=0) -> dead (|MIN|<=1e-7 and |MAX|<=1e-7), forward_only, reverse_only, reversible; growth-forced pass (fraction_of_optimum=0.2) -> essential (0 not in [MIN,MAX]). When the model has both bio1 and bio2 biomasses, run the growth-forced pass once per biomass (MAX{bio1}, MAX{bio2}) and return essential per-biomass plus union (do NOT sum biomasses).
2. find_closed_mode_reactions(model, media=None) — zero-bound all EX_/DM_/SK_ reactions (plus drains from MSModelUtil.exchange_hash()), leave ATPM and biomass unconstrained, run unconstrained run_fva, return reactions still carrying flux, restore all bounds before returning.
3. get_biolog_phenotypes(element=None) — load the committed stash at src/kbutillib/data/biolog_phenotypes.json via importlib.resources (no KBase auth); return all four C/N/S/P MSGrowthPhenotypes sets or one by element. Use MSGrowthPhenotypes.from_dict.
4. refresh_biolog_phenotypes(workspace='KBaseMedia') — enumerate Carbon-*/Nitrogen-*/Sulfate-*/Phosphate- media via kb_ws_utils, build four target_element-constrained MSGrowthPhenotypes sets (target_element + target_element_limit, primary_compounds=the varying source, shared base_media), serialize all four to the committed JSON. Requires KBase auth.
5. simulate_biolog(model, elements=('C','N','S','P'), growth_threshold=0.01) — run MSGrowthPhenotypes.simulate_phenotypes; return functional media per element. When both biomasses exist, simulate per biomass (MAX{bio1}, MAX{bio2}) and report per-biomass plus union. growth as biomass flux 1/h.
6. test_production_potential(model, media=None, threshold=1e-6) — for each cytosolic (_c/_c0, normalize via KBModelUtils._parse_id) metabolite, add a temporary DM_tmp_ demand inside a cobra context manager (with model.model:), MAX{temp_rxn}, record producible if flux>threshold, guarantee NO leaked reactions afterward. (Orchestrator will call it in KBaseMedia/Complete and KBaseMedia/Carbon-D-Glucose.)
7. test_degradation_potential(model, media=None, threshold=1e-6) — symmetric, temporary SK_tmp_ source per cytosolic metabolite, same cleanup guarantee.

Generate and commit the REAL stash file: this task runs in_context on primary-laptop, which HAS KBase auth, so run refresh_biolog_phenotypes against the live KBaseMedia workspace to produce src/kbutillib/data/biolog_phenotypes.json populated with all four C/N/S/P MSGrowthPhenotypes sets, and commit it. Do NOT ship a placeholder. If refresh_biolog_phenotypes fails against KBase, treat it as a build failure and report the error (do not silently fall back to a placeholder).

Tests (tests/, follow existing conftest/fixture conventions; MUST run offline with NO KBase auth — mock get_media/MSGrowthPhenotypes/kb_ws_utils where a network object is needed; bundle a small toy COBRA model + tiny template JSON under tests/fixtures/): cover all seven functions with hand-verified expectations (a known blocked reaction -> dead; a known growth-required reaction -> essential at 0.2; directionality classes mutually exclusive/exhaustive; closed-mode []=loop-free model, flags an injected futile cycle; production finds a known producible and excludes a non-producible; degradation likewise; assert model bounds/reactions unchanged after each sweep) and the Biolog stash round-trip (get_biolog_phenotypes loads + to_dict/from_dict round-trips, four element sets present with target_element set).

Follow existing ms_fba_utils style and logging (self.log_info; gate verbose stage logs behind verbose:bool=False where added). Do not touch ms_reconstruction_utils.py.
parallel_group.success_criteria: src/kbutillib/ms_fba_utils.py contains all seven functions (classify_reactions_by_fva, find_closed_mode_reactions, get_biolog_phenotypes, refresh_biolog_phenotypes, simulate_biolog, test_production_potential, test_degradation_potential) implemented per the PRD's Acceptance Criteria 2-10; classify_reactions_by_fva uses run_fva and never cobra.flux_variability_analysis; the production/degradation sweeps leave model reactions and bounds unchanged (no leaked temporary reactions); src/kbutillib/data/biolog_phenotypes.json is committed and populated with all four C/N/S/P MSGrowthPhenotypes sets generated from the live KBaseMedia workspace (not a placeholder); and `pytest` for the new tests passes with NO KBase credentials available, covering all seven functions plus the Biolog stash round-trip.

parallel_group.prompt:
Implement Phase 2 of the Template Evaluation Suite PRD. READ the PRD first: agent-io/prds/template-evaluation-suite/fullprompt.md (Implementation Decisions, the 'Confront-resolved specifications (round 1)' subsection, and the numbered Acceptance Criteria are binding). Phase 1 (the seven ms_fba_util per-model tests + Biolog stash) is already merged — call those functions, do not reimplement them.

Create a NEW module src/kbutillib/ms_template_utils.py defining MSTemplateUtils(KBModelUtils) and a MSTemplateUtilsImpl, mirroring the MSFBAUtils/MSFBAUtilsImpl pair. Wire it into toolkit.py as KBUtilLib.template and add the optional import in __init__.py under the name MSTemplateUtils (match how ms_fba_utils is wired). Implement:
1. build_full_template_model(template, auto_add_biomass=True) — wrap modelseedpy.MSBuilder.build_full_template_model(template, model_id, index) (this method EXISTS in modelseedpy's MSBuilder). It builds every biomass in template.biomasses and sets bio1 as objective. Detect gram-positive vs gram-negative biomass by the id/name convention in the loaded standard templates (resolve at build time, do not hard-code); if only one cell-wall type is present and auto_add_biomass=True, graft the missing biomass from the corresponding standard ModelSEED V6 template (GramPos/GramNeg).
2. evaluate_template_quality(template, rich_media='KBaseMedia/Complete', minimal_media='KBaseMedia/Carbon-D-Glucose', write_path=None, verbose=False) — build the full template model; run classify_reactions_by_fva in rich+minimal; find_closed_mode_reactions; simulate_biolog; test_production_potential in Complete + Carbon-D-Glucose; test_degradation_potential in Complete. Assemble ONE JSON-serializable report with EXACTLY the canonical keys in Acceptance Criterion 11 (template_metadata; reaction_classes per media rich/minimal each with dead/forward_only/reverse_only/reversible and essential per-biomass+union; closed_mode_reactions; functional_biolog_media per element per-biomass+union; producible_metabolites complete/glucose_minimal; consumable_metabolites complete-only; a count beside every list). If write_path given: directory -> <dir>/report.json + report.md; file stem -> <stem>.json + .md. Log per-stage progress via self.log_info gated behind verbose.
3. render_template_report(report) -> str — PURE function, markdown from the report dict, no recomputation.
4. diff_template_evaluation(model, perturbations, mode='independent', baseline_report=None, write_path=None) — apply perturbations at MODEL level per the schema in Implementation Decisions ({op:add|remove|modify, reaction_id, lower_bound, upper_bound, stoichiometry{met_id:coeff}}); mode='independent' (each vs shared baseline, baseline model left UNMODIFIED) and mode='cumulative' (each vs previous); return a report linking each perturbation to per-category added/removed members and growth gained/lost.

Tests (offline, NO KBase auth; use bundled small template fixture from Phase 1's tests/fixtures or add one): evaluate_template_quality returns all canonical keys with count==len(list) invariants on a small template; diff_template_evaluation for a known-essential-reaction removal produces the expected growth loss / newly-dead reactions in BOTH independent and cumulative modes, and independent mode leaves the baseline model unmodified. Per Acceptance Criteria 1 and 11-16.
parallel_group.success_criteria: src/kbutillib/ms_template_utils.py defines MSTemplateUtils and MSTemplateUtilsImpl, wired as KBUtilLib.template in toolkit.py and importable from the package top level as MSTemplateUtils; build_full_template_model, evaluate_template_quality, render_template_report, and diff_template_evaluation are implemented per the PRD; evaluate_template_quality returns a report with exactly the canonical keys of Acceptance Criterion 11 and count==len invariants; `pytest` passes offline (no KBase auth) covering the report structure and diff_template_evaluation in both independent and cumulative modes (with the baseline model unmodified in independent mode).
